Name: cluster_tpms_blink_timer
EOL Comment: Once this timer expires\, the TPMS light stays on

Name: CAL_logger_high_speed_logger_enable
EOL Comment:
To enable the built-in highspeed data logger\, set this configuration option.

0: disabled (factory defatult)
1: FlexCAN A logging
2: FlexCAN C logging

Name: CAL_ips_request_msg_id_nibble
EOL Comment: 4-bit nibble; IPS CAN request message ID = `(nibble << 4)

Name: CAL_cooling_fan_coolant_temp_threshold
EOL Comment: Coolant temperature above which cooling fan activates

Name: CAL_cooling_fan_air_temp_threshold
EOL Comment: Air temperature threshold for fan activation

Name: CAL_closedloop_o2_breakin_window_hours
EOL Comment: u8_time_hours; maximum engine run time before long-term fuel trim adaptation stops updating

Name: CAL_revlimit_exit_margin
EOL Comment: RPM margin below the soft limit required to fully exit rev limit mode and reset all state

Name: CAL_revlimit_bounce_count_threshold
EOL Comment: The number of rev limit bounces before sustained rev limit mode and aggressive throttle management engage.

Name: CAL_dfco_recovery_enrich_max_rpm
EOL Comment: In short: it suppresses the DFCO re-entry enrichment shot at high RPM where it isn't needed\, preventing over-fueling during high-RPM overrun recovery.

Name: CAL_ign_comp_speed_variation
EOL Comment: ignition compensation indexed by engine speed variation (RPM delta)

Name: CAL_engine_speed_filter_rise_coeff_tour
EOL Comment:
engine_speed_filter_rise_coeff is the IIR low-pass filter coefficient controlling how quickly the tachometer-displayed RPM climbs toward the
  actual engine speed.

Name: CAL_exhaust_flap_position_tour
EOL Comment:
0 = closed          (active control\, eTPU = 10000)
  1 = open            (active control\, eTPU = 0)
  2 = open\, forced    (fail-safe — feature not enabled in
                       COD\, or engine failure flag set)

Name: CAL_cruise_maf_feedforward
EOL Comment:
Role in cruise logic

  When the cruise controller enters \speed acquisition\" mode (flag 0x2000000 in obd_ii_cruise_status)\
column8: it uses this table to know the expected

Name: steady-state airflow at the target speed. It then ramps u16_flow_10mg_s_400014b0 (the working MAF setpoint) down toward that target\
proportional blend when the vehicle is still below the target speed (lines 51007–51027). Once the actual MAF drops below the feedforward
target\
Location: the flag clears and the PID takes over.

Name: This is a classic feedforward / open-loop preload for cruise control — avoids overshoot by starting from a physically-motivated airflow
estimate rather than relying purely on feedback.
